_doc: Module 6 worked examples. Rep responses are quoted verbatim from brief §19 (the canonical deflection table). Contrast example shows what happens when rep invents — buyer trust damages immediately. Anonymised first names only; no PII.

turns.speaker: buyer
turns.text: Before I sign anything — how long does signup take? My bookkeeper hates paperwork.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.text: We run KYC at signup. Takes 10 minutes for the owner. We've done it for 12,000+ businesses; the process is smoother than your bank's.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.text: Ten minutes for the owner. Your bookkeeper doesn't have to touch it.

turns.speaker: buyer
turns.text: OK. Send me a calendar invite for next Tuesday, 11 — I'll do it then.

transcripts.outcome: trust damaged · buyer escalated to procurement

turns.speaker: buyer
turns.text: Are you PSD2 compliant? And how do you handle SCA delegation for our Italian supplier?

turns.speaker: rep
turns.text: Yeah, we're fully PSD2 — and on SCA delegation, I think we use a step-up flow that, uh, depends on the country... I'm pretty sure it's fine for Italy.

turns.speaker: buyer
turns.text: You think? You're pretty sure? That's not an answer I can put in a memo.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.text: Right, no — what I meant was, the platform handles it. Like, technically, the rails do all of it for you.

turns.speaker: buyer
turns.text: Listen — get me someone who actually knows the regulation. I'll loop in procurement; this isn't a sales conversation anymore.

_coach_marks.worked-kyc-verbatim: Rep does not memorise. Rep reads the KYC row out loud — verbatim from §19.
_coach_marks.contrast-psd2-guess: Two improvised words ('I think', 'pretty sure') cost the meeting. The §19 carve-out exists exactly for this question — escalate.
